reason: NOT pinning GitHub-owned actions (decided 2026-06-30, risk-driven, not to-be-green): both repos already SHA-pin all third-party actions (the high-risk tag-hijack class); the remainder is GitHub-owned = Scorecard discounted 20% slice (weight 8:2), lowest-prob compromise, and clean pinning would need a Dependabot updater (SHA-rot) conflicting with the no-Dependabot stance. Squash-only already enforced repo-level; webui ruleset aligned strict=true. See memory project_actions_pinning_decision.

title: Decompose coarse one-FR-per-plugin requirements into sub-FRs (esp. FR-01.10 compliance)
detail: The 14 FRs are one-per-plugin/command (FR-01.10 = the whole /shipwright-compliance plugin), so coverage is trivially 14/14 and never moves, and real capabilities added inside a plugin (Control Grade, honesty gate, CI-security ingestion, SBOM quality, RTM reconciliation) have no specific FR to map to -> they get filed no-FR, depressing strict FR-traceability. Decompose the fat plugin-FRs into sub-FRs (e.g. FR-01.10.x: Control Grade / RTM / SBOM / CI-security ingestion / honesty gate; similarly FR-01.13 adopt, FR-01.11 iterate, FR-01.07 security). Product-modeling task: needs owner input on granularity. Makes coverage meaningful + the grade reflect the real feature surface. Paired with the re-tag follow-up (fr-model-followup).

title: Re-tag mis-filed compliance/security FEATURE events to their parent FRs (clears the traceability decline)
detail: Recent /shipwright-compliance + /shipwright-security FEATURE work was filed change_type=compliance/no-FR though it delivers FR-01.10 (and FR-01.07) functionality, depressing strict FR-tagging to ~10% (vs 18% all-time) -> honesty-gate caps the grade at B. Fix: append event_amended overlays (record_event.py --type event_amended --amends <id>) setting affected_frs + spec_impact=modify on the genuine features. RE-TAG -> FR-01.10 (security ones +FR-01.07): Goodhart Control Grade/honesty gate; AR-10 CI-security ingestion; AR-10 SARIF fallback; AR-04 SBOM quality; AR-05 RTM Reconciled column; Control-Grade anchors/maintainability anchor. STAY no-FR (real infra): scorecard add/fix/remove (CI), mtime drift-detector removal, bloat-baseline tighten, events_log lazy-import doc, ci-security.json data refresh. CAVEAT: spec_impact=modify makes FR-01.10/FR-01.07 behavior-touched -> VERIFY the reconciliation dimension stays green (each touched FR needs a test run at/after its last touch; link a later test event or don't tag the very latest as modify). Expected: recent 3/30 -> ~9/30, decline clears, grade -> A honestly. Pairs with the sub-FR decomposition (fr-model-followup); ideally do the decomposition first so features map to specific sub-FRs.

title: Measure patch/diff coverage (were the CHANGED lines tested?), not just pass-rate
detail: Reviewer comment B: '3618/3618 green' is pass-rate, not coverage — it says nothing about whether AI-added code is even executed. Killer case for AI-first: AI writes code + a trivial test that misses the risky branch; pass-rate stays green, new code untested. The Test Completeness Ledger is an author-CLAIMED checklist, not instrumented. Today CI runs plain pytest (no --cov). BUILD: pytest --cov -> coverage.xml -> diff-cover against the PR merge-base = % of CHANGED lines covered; surface as (a) a CI gate (warn/fail under threshold) AND (b) an input to the Control Grade Test-health dimension (note: the W4 verifier already reads shipwright_test_results.json coverage.total but the field is never populated -> SKIP). SCOPE/caveats: touches CI across ALL plugins (each has its own pytest step) + a coverage-combine step + a schema field + grade wiring -> its own iterate (biggest of the A/B/C/D set, why it wasn't bundled into #296). Generic repo-grader story (arbitrary languages) is harder -> do monorepo dogfood first, defer the generic case. Central to the 'control over AI code' thesis.

title: github_triage artifact-ingest counts inline-suppressed Semgrep findings as live, inflating gh-security triage and blocking auto-resolve
detail: shared/scripts/github_triage/producer.py security_artifact_action_unit() sets artifact_total = len(findings) with no suppression filter. On the artifact-ingest path (GHAS Code Scanning unavailable, cs_alerts is None) this counts inline-suppressed Semgrep findings (SARIF results carrying suppressions:[{kind:inSource}]) as live, inflating the gh-security:<owner>/<repo> action-unit count. Since the count never reaches 0, resolve_stale never auto-dismisses the item and it re-appears after every manual dismiss. Fix: count only results where (.suppressions // []) | length == 0 before len(), so a fully-suppressed scan yields 0 findings -> producer returns None -> auto-resolve gate fires. Add a regression test with a SARIF fixture containing suppressed results. Found during a shipwright-webui security review of CI run 28407935303 (12 inline-suppressed FPs surfaced as 12 low).
